codeReview.summary: The change makes reviewer inbox entry more testable and keeps role/node filtering explicit, but it does not actually verify or implement the promised workbench hydration continuity after task selection.

issues.description: The new hydration coverage only asserts that `setCurrentTask(task)` is called with a fixture that already contains `formId`, attachments, and workflow history. It never exercises the real hydration path inside `useReviewStore.setCurrentTask`, so M2-003, M2-008, and INT-006 remain unproven: regressions where selection fails to preserve or reload shared context would still pass this suite.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `review-entry-worker` skill says step 9 must perform live reviewer smoke validation on `http://127.0.0.1:3101/`, but this worker treated closing an unused browser session as sufficient and still reported `followedProcedure: true`.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff interactiveChecks only record `agent-browser --session "41dae33f60da" close`; no task-selection smoke was launched. The skill file `.factory/skills/review-entry-worker/SKILL.md` step 9 requires selecting a task and validating a workflow path or nearest smoke equivalent.

summary: Fail: inbox filtering and helper extraction are reasonable, but the feature does not actually prove or cover the real reviewer workbench hydration continuity it claims to satisfy.
